headline: CAT Stock Powers Dow Jones On Fastest Growth Since '21
summary: Caterpillar steamrolled first-quarter earnings forecasts early Thursday thanks to data center strength and a broadening industrial recovery.  In the earnings statement, Caterpillar CEO Joe Creed highlighted "robust order activity" and a "record backlog" as evidence of "the strength of our business and our focus on solving our customers' toughest challenges."  Estimates: Analysts expected Caterpillar to post Q1 earnings per share of $4.65, up 9% from a year ago, as revenue rises 16% to $16.527 billion, according to FactSet.

headline: Cat Financial Reports First-Quarter 2026 Results
summary: Cat Financial reported first-quarter 2026 revenues of $947 million, an increase of $87 million, or 10%, compared with $860 million in the first quarter of 2025. The increase in revenues was primarily due to a favorable impact from higher average earning assets. First-quarter 2026 profit was $144 million, an increase of $14 million, or 11%, compared with $130 million profit in the first quarter of 2025.

headline: Caterpillar Taps PROPWR And Data Centers For New Power Growth
summary: Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT) has entered a new framework agreement with ProPetro’s PROPWR unit to supply high efficiency power generation equipment. The agreement targets power solutions for data centers, oil and gas operations, and industrial customers. Caterpillar is also ramping up its presence in data center power infrastructure, aligning with rising demand for reliable digital capacity. Caterpillar is best known for heavy equipment, but its energy and power systems segment is a key part of...
